appraisal_events.evidence: « je pris pour point de départ de mes autres idées la certitude … de sa culpabilité »; « Il fallait que je vécusse … avec l’idée de ses fautes »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator locally adopts the belief that Albertine was guilty (of ‘fautes’/‘trahisons’), using this certainty as a stage in his healing. He notes this ‘certitude’ is often later contradicted, so the text presents the blame as provisional rather than factually endorsed.

status_effects.explanation: Within this passage, Albertine’s standing in the narrator’s eyes declines because he treats her culpability as a working certainty, even as the narration frames it as a therapeutic, possibly inaccurate belief.

ambiguities: The narrator frames the ‘certitude’ of Albertine’s guilt as a healing phase and admits it was often contradicted; dream scenes also mix her being alive and dead, complicating how firmly the blame should be taken.
